# tokens: # Behavior dispatch spec — covers `compare` and `canon` capabilities
# installed via the unified `reg` word.
#   reg compare/q  (fn [[T T] [Integer] [body]])
#   reg canon/q    (fn [[T]   [String]  [body]])
#   reg jsonify/q  (fn [[T]   [Any]     [body]])
# `jsonify` projects a value into a Node or Scalar (the AQL data
# subset) rather than into a JSON string — callers compose with a
# separate encoder when they need wire-format JSON. The default
# (when no jsonify is registered) returns the value unchanged.
# Three dispatch layers:
#   1. CORE comparators / canonisers — kernel scalar Behaviors
#      (Integer, Decimal, Number cross-promotion, String, Boolean,
#      Atom) hold the canonical `Compare` and `Format` impls.
#   2. NATIVE comparators / canonisers — lang-layer domain types
#      (Date, Instant, ClkDuration, …) attach Compare / Format to
#      their existing FormatBehavior in lang/engine/native_temporal.go.
#   3. USER-DEFINED — `reg compare/q` and `reg canon/q` install AQL
#      bodies via lang/engine/native_reg.go's userBehavior wrapper.
#      Both behaviors can coexist on one type — the wrapper carries
#      multiple capability slots.
# 1a. CORE compare: kernel scalar Comparers
# Integer ordering — numberCompareBehavior on TNumber handles
# Integer-Integer pairs via the lattice walk plus Decimal pairs.
2 5 lt
5 2 lt
5 5 lt
5 5 lte
5 5 gte
5 2 gt
# Decimal ordering — same dispatch path, AsNumber coerces.
# Cross-type Integer + Decimal: LCA = Number; the Comparer on Number
# handles the entire numeric branch — no per-pair Behavior.
3.0 3 lte
# String lexicographic ordering.
'apple' 'banana' lt
'banana' 'apple' lt
'same' 'same' lt
# Boolean: false < true.
false true lt
true false lt
true true lt
# Atom: lex order by name.
red/q green/q lt
green/q red/q lt
red/q red/q lt
# Cross-branch (Integer vs String) — no shared Comparer below the
# root, so dispatch errors rather than silently coercing.
1 'a' lt
1 true lt
# 1b. CORE canon: kernel default rendering through CanonValue
# Each scalar value renders to its canonical AQL source form (the
# round-trippable representation produced by eng.CanonValue).
'hello'
true
false
red/q
# 2a. NATIVE compare: predefined domain Comparers
# Constructed via the aql:time module — the test runner pre-installs
# its exports so rows can use `time.unix`, `time.seconds`, … directly.
(0 time.unix) (100 time.unix) lt
(100 time.unix) (0 time.unix) lt
(50 time.unix) (50 time.unix) lt
(50 time.unix) (50 time.unix) lte
(0 time.unix) (100 time.unix) gt
(5 time.seconds) (10 time.seconds) lt
(10 time.seconds) (5 time.seconds) lt
# 2b. NATIVE canon: domain Format Behaviors
# Each native type's FormatBehavior produces a domain-specific render
# (Instant in RFC3339Nano, ClkDuration as a Go duration string, …).
0 time.unix
100 time.unix
5 time.seconds
# 3a. USER-DEFINED compare: reg compare/q (fn [[T T] [Integer] [body]])
# `reg compare/q` installs a Comparer on type T. The body receives the
# operands as the defs `a` and `b`; the trailing Integer determines
# ordering: negative → a<b, zero → equal, positive → a>b.
# Ascending by age.
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob lt
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) bob alice lt
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) def bob (make Person {age:25}) alice bob gt
# Descending: swap operands.
type Item object {n:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub]]) def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i1 i9 lt
type Item object {n:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(b 'n' get) (a 'n' get) sub]]) def i1 (make Item {n:1}) def i9 (make Item {n:9}) i9 i1 lt
# Self-equality.
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lt
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) def alice (make Person {age:30}) alice alice lte
# Sig validation: heterogeneous input params rejected for compare.
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Integer] [Integer] [42]])
expected: ERROR:both params must be the same type

# tokens: # Return type validation.
type Person object {age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [String] ['oops']])
# Builtin type override refused.
reg compare/q (fn [[Integer Integer] [Integer] [0]])
# Unknown behavior name.
reg bogus/q (fn [[Integer Integer] [Integer] [0]])
# Body error propagates through the Comparer chain.
type Faulty object {x:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Faulty Faulty] [Integer] [quux]]) def v1 (make Faulty {x:1}) def v2 (make Faulty {x:2}) v1 v2 lt
# 3b. USER-DEFINED canon: reg canon/q (fn [[T] [String] [body]])
# `reg canon/q` installs a Format-via-canon Behavior on T. The body
# receives the value as the def `a` and returns a String — that
# String becomes the value's canonical render (via Value.String,
# eng.Canon, every error message).
# Render Person as just its name field. The body returns the raw
# string contents — that becomes the canonical render for Person
# values (Canon → CanonValue's default fallthrough → Value.String →
# userBehavior.Format → body output).
type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Multi-field canon body — string concat plus a fixed suffix.
type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get) ' (person)' add]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Canon coexists with compare on the same type — both wrap one
# userBehavior; installing the second does not lose the first.

type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) def alice (make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}) def bob (make Person {name:'Bob' age:25}) alice bob lt
note: compare still works after canon installed

# tokens: type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
note: canon still works after compare installed

# tokens: # Sig validation: canon must take exactly 1 param.
type Person object {name:String} reg canon/q (fn [[Person Person] [String] ['x']])
# Return-type validation.
type Person object {name:String} reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [Integer] [42]])
# 4. JSONIFY: project a value into a Node or Scalar
# `reg jsonify/q (fn [[T] [Any] [body]])` installs a projection on T;
# the body's return value is a Node or Scalar (data-shape, NOT a
# JSON-encoded string). Two access words:
#   - `tonode VALUE` — runs the projection, returns the Node/Scalar
#     directly. Used here for spec assertions on the body output.
#   - `jsonify VALUE` — runs the projection THEN serialises via the
#     JSON encoder. Existing word — the customisation hook now flows
#     through it transparently.
# With no jsonify behavior registered for the type, `tonode` returns
# the value unchanged (Map / List / scalar are already data-shaped).
# 4a. Default pass-through for native data shapes.
5 tonode
'hello' tonode
true tonode
[1 2 3] tonode
{a:1 b:2} tonode
# 4b. NATIVE pass-through — domain types with no jsonify behavior
# attached also pass through unchanged.
0 time.unix tonode
# 4c. USER-DEFINED — a Person with a custom jsonify projects to a
# Map with renamed / filtered fields. The body returns a Map (Node);
# `tonode` exposes that Map directly so the spec can assert on its
# structure without going through the JSON-string encoder.
type Person object {name:String age:Integer secret:String} reg jsonify/q (fn [[Person] [Any] [{n: (a 'name' get), age: (a 'age' get)}]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30 secret:'hush'} tonode
# Body can return Scalar, not just Node — projection is free to
# collapse the whole instance to a primitive.
type Money object {cents:Integer} reg jsonify/q (fn [[Money] [Any] [(a 'cents' get)]]) make Money {cents:1234} tonode
# Compose with compare + canon on the same type — three capability
# slots coexist on one userBehavior wrapper.

type Item object {n:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Item Item] [Integer] [(a 'n' get) (b 'n' get) sub]]) reg canon/q (fn [[Item] [String] [(a 'n' get) ' (item)' add]]) reg jsonify/q (fn [[Item] [Any] [{value: (a 'n' get)}]]) def i (make Item {n:42}) i tonode
# Sig validation — jsonify takes exactly 1 param.
type Foo object {x:Integer} reg jsonify/q (fn [[Foo Foo] [Any] [42]])
